title: Chicken Mole with Poblanos
description: A classic Mexican dish with a rich and complex sauce made from chocolate and spices. This recipe pairs perfectly with roasted poblano peppers.
instructions: Roast the poblano peppers by placing them on a baking sheet and cooking for 30 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.
Remove the peppers from the oven and let them cool. Peel off the skin, remove the seeds, and chop the flesh into small pieces.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the mole paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, chocolate chips, almonds, cumin, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the roasted poblano peppers to the skillet and stir to combine.
Serve the chicken and sauce hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
